title: elymus elymoides subsp. californicus
intro: Elymus elymoides subsp. californicus, also known as California wildrye, is a subspecies of the Elymus elymoides grass, a native wild grass to North America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. The health benefits of California wildrye include its potential to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. California wildrye can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use.
